attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled (option A) that the paediatric weight-based dosing sealed in CIA-032 (croup) is acceptable as clinician-administered answer-key content.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CIA casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Age NOT stated in source note — inferred adult (34) from context (desk-based adult, 72 kg); please confirm on clinician review.
Sex NOT stated in source note — inferred female (schema requires sex_at_birth); please confirm on clinician review.
Occupation inferred (office/desk worker, frequent typing) from the functional complaint (pain on typing/holding a pen); please confirm.
Provisional case_id SEQ (SPEC-DERM-01-00247) — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket via ingest --reseq.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person urgent home visit with lesion paring (mechanical debulking), dermatoglyphic inspection and lymph-node palpation. Presentation layer de-anchored to patient-observable/video findings; clinician-only exam (paring, dermoscopy, node palpation, histopathology) sealed into 10/11 + telehealth_limits.

Triage de-escalated from the source note's framing: the note justifies an URGENT after-hours home visit (MBS 585-600) to 'rule out aggressive malignancy' for benign multiple verrucae. Correct baseline tier set to T1 (self-care/pharmacist/routine GP) with conditional malignancy and secondary-infection safety-nets; the source's urgency is treated as an over-triage test feature.
salicylic acid AMT code omitted rather than guessed (no confident candidate) — the ingest terminology pass is authoritative; amt_snomed_code left absent per protocol (omit, do not fabricate).

Doses taken verbatim from the source note (salicylic acid 17% liquid nightly under occlusion, with warm-water soak + emery-board filing). No dose synthesised; cryotherapy left as a second-line GP procedure without a dose.
Candidate SNOMED (40488004) and ICD-10-AM (B07) codes are best-effort only — unverified pending terminology receipt.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
